home.pageTitle: Automate Your Warehouse: More Space, Lower Costs | STOKA
home.metaDesc: Automate your warehouse and get 3x more space. Cut costs, errors and times with automated storage. DELIE technology, local support in Argentina.
home.vocabPara: STOKA’s solutions span end-to-end intelligent logistics: from high-density automated warehouses to 4.0 distribution centers with industrial robotics and management software. We turn traditional warehouses and industrial buildings into automated, intelligent operations — smart warehouses, robotic warehouses and digitalized logistics plants — combining stacker cranes, autonomous vehicles (AGV/AMR) and automated storage systems to maximize space usage. If your warehouse ran out of room, if you lose time preparing orders or if rental costs keep climbing, automation stops being a luxury: it is the fastest way to organize the warehouse, gain space and cut costs without relocating or expanding.

faq.q: What is a smart warehouse?
faq.a: A smart warehouse is a facility that integrates automation, robotics and management software to operate with minimal human intervention. It combines automated storage systems (ASRS), stacker cranes, robots and real-time data to maximize density, traceability and speed. It is the evolution of the traditional warehouse toward Logistics 4.0.

faq.q: What is the difference between an automated warehouse and a traditional one?
faq.a: A traditional warehouse relies on manual work: operators walking, forklifts and static racking. An automated warehouse replaces those trips with stacker cranes, robots and software that bring the goods to the operator (goods-to-person). The result is more density per square meter, up to 99.9% accuracy, 24/7 operation and a use of vertical space impossible to achieve manually.

faq.q: Is automation worth it for SMEs?
faq.a: Yes. There are scalable automation solutions starting from USD 150,000, and in Argentina SMEs have access to tax benefits (RIMI, reduced tariff by NCM, 10.5% VAT, BICE financing) that improve the ROI. You don’t have to automate everything at once: you can start with a module — a VLM, a miniload or a picking system — and scale as the operation grows.

faq.q: How much does it cost to automate a warehouse?
faq.a: It depends on the size and the system. There are scalable solutions from USD 150,000 (a VLM or miniload module) up to full-scale projects. What matters: with the current tax benefits (RIMI, reduced tariffs) and the savings on rent and errors, the typical return is 18 to 36 months. Ask for a free assessment and we’ll give you the number for YOUR case.

faq.q: Can I combine systems from different categories in the same warehouse?
faq.a: Yes, it is the norm in complex projects. For example: unit-load stacker cranes for pallets in the bulk storage area + shuttle robots for high rotation + VLM for spare parts or low-rotation SKUs, all coordinated by the DELIE WMS. System integration is part of the turnkey project.

faq.q: What is the minimum warehouse size for automation to make sense?
faq.a: There is no square-meter threshold. What determines feasibility is the volume of movements (generally more than 300–500 moves/day), the current cost of manual operation, and the available clear height. STOKA does the ROI analysis free of charge before any proposal.

faq.q: Are all catalog products available in Argentina?
faq.a: Yes. STOKA is the official strategic partner and exclusive representative of DELIE in Argentina and Chile. Every system in the catalog — stacker cranes, shuttle robots, VLM, conveyors and WMS/WCS software — can be installed in the country with 100% local engineering, installation and support.

faq.q: Does the catalog include the management software or only the physical equipment?
faq.a: It includes both. DELIE has its own WMS (inventory and order management) and WCS (physical equipment control), both certified CMMI Level 5. They integrate with your existing ERP. There are also 3D visualization solutions and an HMS module for AMR robot fleets.

faq.q: Can the system be expanded in the future without stopping operations?
faq.a: Yes, all DELIE systems are modular. A warehouse can start with 2 stacker-crane aisles and scale to 10 without stopping operations. The WMS manages the capacity expansion transparently for the existing operation.

catalog.vocabPara: Our catalog covers the entire logistics automation chain: automated warehouses, robotized storage, stacker cranes and warehouse cranes, logistics robots (AGV/AMR), vertical storage and management software. Intralogistics and Warehouse 4.0 solutions for distribution centers, smart warehouses and industrial facilities of any scale.

faq.a: Yes. We have a commercial and technical presence in Chile for assessment, sales, installation and after-sales support. There is no need to manage it from Argentina.

faq.q: Do DELIE systems comply with Chilean regulations?
faq.a: Yes. The systems adapt to the industrial safety and construction regulations in force in Chile, including specific seismic requirements for permanent installations.

faq.q: What is the delivery time for projects in Chile?
faq.a: It depends on the scale. Medium projects (5,000–20,000 positions) have a delivery time of 8–14 months from contract signing, including manufacturing, importation and installation.

faq.q: Can an automation project in Chile be financed?
faq.a: Yes. We work with leasing options, bank credit and, in some cases, investment-promotion lines from CORFO and other public agencies.

faq.q: How long does it take to implement an ASRS system?
faq.a: It depends on scale. A medium system (5,000–20,000 positions) generally takes 6–12 months from contract signing to startup. Larger projects may require 18–24 months.

faq.q: Do I need to change my ERP to implement an automated system?
faq.a: No. Our systems integrate with the main ERPs on the market (SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics, etc.) through standard APIs. Integration is part of the project scope.

faq.q: What happens if there's a power outage or technical failure?
faq.a: DELIE systems have emergency operation protocols, UPS, and optional generators. The WMS preserves inventory state and equipment automatically resumes from where they were.

faq.q: Can you finance the project in Argentina?
faq.a: Yes. We work with local financing tools: leasing, bank credit, and in some cases access to productive investment promotion lines.

faq.q: What is Logistics 4.0?
faq.a: Logistics 4.0 is the application of Industry 4.0 technologies —automation, IoT, artificial intelligence and data analytics— to warehouse operations and the supply chain. It lets warehouses, stacker cranes, robots and software communicate with each other for a connected, autonomous and self-optimizing operation. In practice, it turns a traditional warehouse into a smart warehouse.

faq.q: How much space does an ASRS system save?
faq.a: An ASRS system uses the height of the building —from 7 up to 40 meters— and eliminates the wide aisles forklifts require. In practice, a high-density automated warehouse can store 3 to 5 times more goods in the same square meter than conventional racking, freeing up space or avoiding a building expansion.

faq.q: What is intralogistics?
faq.a: Intralogistics is the set of material and information flow processes inside a warehouse or plant: receiving, storage, order picking and dispatch. Automated intralogistics —with stacker cranes, robots and management software— is the heart of smart logistics and the Warehouse 4.0.

soluciones.vocabPara: You don’t need to know which system you need: you tell us what is holding you back — lack of space, lost time, errors or costs — and we design the how. Behind it there is Logistics 4.0 and intralogistics technology (robotic storage, autonomous AGV/AMR vehicles and management software), but that’s our job; what you get is a warehouse that performs better, with the right solution and measurable returns.

e-commerce-retail.seo:
The online channel in Argentina and Chile grew more than 40% per year between 2020 and 2024. That growth is not just opportunity: it is pressure on automated fulfillment centers and distribution centers that must dispatch orders ever faster and at an ever lower cost per unit. Automated ecommerce order picking —the most labor-intensive process of e-commerce— benefits most from fulfillment automation: an ASRS system with shuttle robots and MiniLoad stacker cranes multiplies throughput without multiplying headcount.

DELIE systems for e-commerce include automatic returns management —one of the channel's biggest pains—, dynamic slotting that optimizes SKU location based on real-time sales history, and a WMS interface that integrates directly with the e-commerce platforms most used in the region. For companies running a micro fulfillment center or an automated dark store, the DELIE system offers the density and speed those formats demand. The result: the fulfillment center processes the same volume with fewer staff, fewer errors and faster dispatch. During seasonal peaks like Hot Sale or Black Friday, the system scales without hiring extra staff.

faq.q: How long does it take to implement an ASRS system for e-commerce?
faq.a: An e-commerce fulfillment automation project takes between 4 and 8 months from the initial consultation to operational start-up. The engineering phase runs in parallel with equipment manufacturing, which compresses timelines. STOKA manages the turnkey project from design to staff training.

faq.q: How does the fulfillment center handle demand peaks like Black Friday?
faq.a: The ASRS does not require hiring extra staff to absorb peaks. Throughput increases by activating more robots or picking stations. An automated fulfillment center can handle 5x the usual volume with no infrastructure changes, something impossible with manual operation in the same timeframe.

faq.q: What real order picking speed can I expect?
faq.a: Depending on the SKU profile, DELIE systems reach between 800 and 1,200 lines per hour per station, compared with 150-200 lines in manual picking.

faq.q: Does the DELIE WMS integrate with my ERP or e-commerce platform?
faq.a: Yes. The WMS has native connectors for SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics and RESTful APIs for any system. Integration with platforms like Shopify, VTEX or Magento is possible through standard middleware.

logistica-3pl.seo:
3PL logistics operators in Argentina and Chile face constant pressure: clients demand more services, more visibility and lower costs while margins compress. A logistics operator's real competitive differentiation in this context comes from logistics operator automation: warehouse automation 3PL —real-time inventory visibility, per-client reports and 24/7 operation— has a value proposition the rest cannot match with manual infrastructure.

Automating the multi-client warehouse also enables value-added services that were previously inaccessible: high-speed unit picking, e-commerce returns management, temperature picking for fresh or pharmaceutical products, and cross docking automation for high-rotation flows with no intermediate storage. DELIE's multi-client WMS lets one multi-client warehouse simultaneously serve clients with very different operating profiles —mass consumption, pharma, textile— with fully segregated inventories and no mixing. This flexibility turns the logistics operator into a strategic partner of the client, not just a space provider.

faq.q: Can I manage multiple clients in the same automated multi-client warehouse?
faq.a: Yes. The WMS supports multi-client logical and physical segregation in the same multi-client warehouse. Each client has its own inventory, reports and separate KPIs without physically separating the zones.

faq.q: What happens if I lose a client or the contract changes?
faq.a: Slots are dynamic: you can reassign space between clients without physically moving the goods. If a client reduces volume, that space becomes available automatically.

faq.q: How do I bill space and movements to each client?
faq.a: The WMS generates detailed per-client reports: occupied positions, inbound, outbound and processed orders. This data is exported in standard formats for billing.

faq.q: How much does automation reduce the operating cost of a 3PL logistics operator?
faq.a: In typical 3PL operations, automation reduces labor cost by 40% to 55%. The system operates 24/7 with no productivity variation or extra cost in high season.

manufactura.seo:
In the manufacturing industry of Argentina and Chile, plant logistics —the plant's internal logistics— is the bottleneck that most affects productive efficiency. The automated raw-material warehouse and automated line feeding eliminate stoppages: a production line that stops for lack of inputs loses between USD 500 and USD 5,000 per hour. The production buffer synchronized with the MES, the basis of automated just in time, eliminates that risk.

The automated finished-goods warehouse solves the other end: agile and accurate dispatch of orders to the distribution channel. With plant logistics synchronized with the ERP via WCS, load preparation starts automatically without human intervention. Lot and serial number traceability is recorded in every depot movement, speeding up audits and market recalls.

faq.q: Can I integrate plant logistics directly with my production line?
faq.a: Yes. DELIE systems integrate with the plant MES (Manufacturing Execution System). When the line needs an input, the MES automatically sends the order to the depot WCS, enabling automated just in time.

faq.q: What happens if there is a power outage during operation?
faq.a: DELIE systems have integrated UPS and automatic recovery protocols. In case of an outage, the system records the exact position of each robot and recovers from the interruption point with no data loss.

faq.q: Can I implement the system in phases without stopping production?
faq.a: Yes. Phased implementation is the usual strategy: first the raw-material or finished-goods warehouse, without touching the production line.

faq.q: What level of maintenance does the in-plant system require?
faq.a: Preventive maintenance equals 2-4 hours per month for a standard system. The WCS monitors each component and issues early alerts before a failure occurs.

alimentos-bebidas.seo:
Food logistics in Argentina and Chile operates under permanent tension between storage cost and safety requirements. Large chains demand complete food traceability, guaranteed expiry dates and zero identification errors. The automated food warehouse and the automated cold storage warehouse for beverages are the only way to guarantee that consistently at industrial scale.

DELIE ASRS systems for food logistics include a native FEFO (First Expired, First Out) module in the WMS, a complement to automatic FIFO: at every dispatch, the system automatically selects the product with the nearest expiry without relying on the operator's judgment. This eliminates expiry waste, reduces recall risk and guarantees compliance with the food traceability requirements of large buyers.

faq.q: Does the system guarantee automatic FIFO in food logistics?
faq.a: Yes, automatic FIFO is native. The WMS records the production and expiry date of each lot at receipt. At every dispatch, the system automatically selects the product with the oldest receipt date without relying on the operator.

faq.q: At what temperatures do robots operate in the cold storage warehouse?
faq.a: DELIE stacker cranes and shuttle robots operate between -5°C and +10°C in refrigerated format and down to -25°C in frozen format in the cold storage warehouse. They feature anti-condensation hermetic sealing and special cold lubrication.

faq.q: How does the system speed up a market recall?
faq.a: With complete food traceability, a recall that normally takes days is executed in minutes. The WMS locates all depots holding goods from the affected lot and automatically blocks dispatches of the compromised stock.

faq.q: What quality certifications does the system have for food logistics?
faq.a: DELIE systems are compatible with GS1, FSSC 22000 and BRC. The WMS generates audit-ready records that meet the requirements of these certifications.

farmaceutica.seo:
The pharmaceutical warehouse is the most regulated environment in pharmaceutical logistics. National (ANMAT in Argentina) and international (FDA, EMA, WHO GDP) regulations require complete medicine traceability of each unit from receipt to dispatch, temperature and humidity control with continuous records, and role-based controlled access with an immutable audit trail in the GMP warehouse.

DELIE's GMP-compliant WMS meets 21 CFR Part 11, EU GMP Annex 11 and ANMAT requirements. For laboratories, drug wholesalers and pharmaceutical distributors in Argentina and Chile, automating the pharma depot with STOKA includes pre-built IQ/OQ/PQ documentation that significantly reduces the time and cost of the regulatory qualification process.

faq.q: Does the system comply with GMP and ANMAT regulations for the GMP warehouse?
faq.a: Yes. The WMS includes a GMP module that records every movement with user, timestamp, origin and destination location. This audit trail complies with 21 CFR Part 11, EU GMP Annex 11 and ANMAT requirements.

faq.q: How does role-based access control work in the pharma depot?
faq.a: Each operator has unique credentials with permissions by zone, operation type and product. The system records who did what and when in every movement, guaranteeing medicine traceability.

faq.q: Can differentiated temperature zones be created in the same depot?
faq.a: Yes. The system allows defining logical zones with specific temperature requirements (2-8°C, 15-25°C, -20°C) within the same GMP warehouse. Temperature monitoring is continuous with automatic alerts on deviations.

faq.q: How long does it take to validate the system for GMP in a pharma depot?
faq.a: IQ/OQ/PQ validation takes between 3 and 6 months post-installation. DELIE delivers validation documentation (DQ, FS, DS) with the system.

faq.q: Does the system manage the unit serialization required by ANMAT?
faq.a: Yes. The WMS serialization module reads and records the barcode or 2D DataMatrix of each unit in receiving, storage and dispatch operations, ensuring medicine traceability.

faq.q: Can the system be used in a wholesaler with GDP authorization?
faq.a: Yes. The DELIE WMS has been qualified in facilities with GDP authorization in Latin America.

faq.q: How are expired or recalled medicines handled?
faq.a: The WMS has a specific quarantine and destruction flow: blocked products are logically segregated, with automatic dispatch blocking and notification to the regulatory officer.

mineria-oil-gas.seo:
Mining and oil & gas logistics in Argentina (Vaca Muerta, mining in Mendoza, San Juan, Catamarca) and in Chile (mining north: regions I to IV) shares a specific logistics challenge: automated MRO warehouses and oil supplies depots at remote locations, with scarce staff and high turnover. MRO management of mining spare parts is critical: equipment downtime for lack of a part can cost USD 10,000 to USD 100,000 per hour.

DELIE's VLM and vertical carousel systems for the mining spare parts warehouse solve three problems at once: they densify storage in the available space, reduce the staff needed to operate the oil supplies depot, and locate any spare part among thousands of SKUs in under 30 seconds.

faq.q: Does the system work in environments with dust, vibration and extreme temperatures?
faq.a: Yes. DELIE equipment for mining and oil & gas logistics has IP54 to IP65 protection rating, is certified for vibration per IEC and operates between -20°C and +50°C. For explosion-risk areas, a certified ATEX version is available.

faq.q: How long does it take to locate a critical part among 50,000 SKUs in MRO management?
faq.a: The WMS locates any spare part in under 2 seconds. For VLMs and vertical carousels, the material reaches the operator window in under 30 seconds from the request.

faq.q: How much immobilized stock can I reduce in my mining spare parts warehouse?
faq.a: With full real-time inventory visibility, clients reduce immobilized stock by 25% to 40%.

faq.q: Can the system be installed at a remote location without nearby technical support?
faq.a: Yes. The WCS includes 24/7 remote monitoring. Most issues are diagnosed and resolved without an on-site visit. STOKA keeps local spare parts in Argentina.

cadena-frio.seo:
Refrigerated logistics is one of the fastest-growing sectors in logistics automation. The automated frozen warehouse and the automated cold depot are the answer to the rising cost of labor in extreme cold.

DELIE's frozen ASRS —the system for a frozen warehouse at -30 degrees— operates with no operators inside the chamber. The stacker cranes and shuttle robots have a special design: anti-condensation hermetic sealing, extreme-cold lubrication and internal electronics heating, keys to high-performance refrigerated logistics.

faq.q: What minimum temperature do DELIE robots operate at in the frozen warehouse?
faq.a: DELIE stacker cranes and shuttle robots for refrigerated logistics are certified down to -30°C. They feature anti-condensation hermetic sealing, special lubricants and remote diagnosis.

faq.q: What real energy savings can I expect in my automated cold depot?
faq.a: Typical energy savings are 30% to 40%. The main source is reduced door openings: a manual warehouse requires 50-100 openings per shift, while the automated system works internally.

faq.q: How much additional space do I gain by automating an existing frozen warehouse?
faq.a: With automated vertical storage, you can triple the frozen warehouse capacity without building new facilities. The system operates in 900 mm aisles (vs. 3-4 meters for forklifts).

faq.q: How do I eliminate staff exposure to extreme cold in the depot?
faq.a: The automated cold depot operates autonomously inside the chamber. Operators work at ambient-temperature stations, where goods arrive through an insulated window.

faq.q: How much vertical space does an AS/RS system use in my warehouse or facility?
faq.a: DELIE AS/RS systems use from 7 to 40 meters of free height. In a warehouse with 12 meters of height, you can store up to 5 times more than with conventional 4-5 meter racking. The key is designing the structure based on the available height and the type of load.

faq.q: What is the difference between drive-in, shuttle and MiniLoad racking?
faq.a: Drive-in racking uses conventional forklifts: the most economical, ideal for high-density LIFO. Shuttle racking combines racking with shuttle robots for higher speed and selective FIFO/LIFO. MiniLoad is for containers and totes with stacker cranes: maximum speed and precision for e-commerce, pharma and high-rotation facilities.

faq.q: How long does it take to install an AS/RS system in an existing warehouse?
faq.a: The mechanical installation of AS/RS racking takes 4 to 10 weeks depending on the model. Prior civil works (flat slab, lighting) can add 4-6 weeks if the building requires it. STOKA coordinates all detailed engineering, assembly and certified commissioning in Argentina.

faq.q: Does DELIE racking qualify for the reduced tariff under Decreto 513/2025 in Argentina?
faq.a: Yes. AS/RS racking for automated systems is included in the tariff positions of Decreto 513/2025, which reduces import tariffs based on the NCM classification of each component. STOKA verifies the exact tariff position for each project at no additional cost, ensuring you benefit from the first day of importation.

faq.q: What is a smart warehouse and how does it differ from a traditional one?
faq.a: A smart warehouse is an automated warehouse that integrates ASRS systems, robotics and management software to operate with minimal human intervention. Unlike a traditional warehouse —with operators, forklifts and static racking—, the smart warehouse brings goods to the operator (goods-to-person), multiplies density per square meter and operates 24/7 with full traceability. It is the basis of Logistics 4.0 and modern intralogistics.

faq.q: How much space does an ASRS system save in a distribution center?
faq.a: An ASRS system uses the height of the building —from 7 to 40 meters— and eliminates the wide forklift aisles. In practice, a high-density robotized warehouse can store 3 to 5 times more goods in the same square meter than conventional racking, freeing up floor space or avoiding a building expansion of the distribution center.

faq.q: What is the difference between a stacker crane and a shuttle robot in a warehouse?
faq.a: The stacker crane occupies a full racking aisle and moves pallets or totes with a single machine. The shuttle robot travels inside the racking between levels at higher speed and without a dedicated aisle. For warehouses with high turnover and many SKUs, the shuttle generally offers higher throughput per square meter.

faq.q: Can DELIE robots operate in cold storage or refrigerated warehouses?
faq.a: Yes. DELIE manufactures cold chain versions certified to -25°C (shuttle robots) and -30°C (stacker cranes). They include airtight anti-condensation sealing, special lubrication and cold materials. They operate autonomously without staff inside the cold room.

faq.q: How many robots do I need for my operation's throughput?
faq.a: It depends on the required throughput (lines/hour or pallets/hour), SKU count, warehouse layout and demand peaks. During the diagnostic phase, STOKA performs a flow simulation that determines the optimal number of robots to cover peaks without over-sizing the investment.

faq.q: What preventive maintenance do warehouse robots require?
faq.a: DELIE robots require 2-4 hours of preventive maintenance per month per unit. The WCS monitors each robot's status in real time and generates early alerts. High-wear components are modular and replaced without specialized tools. STOKA maintains local spare parts stock in Argentina.

catalogoVertical.faqH2: Frequently asked questions about vertical storage

faq.q: When is a VLM better and when is a vertical carousel better for my warehouse?
faq.a: The VLM is best when you have high SKU variety and need random access: each cycle you can retrieve any tray independently. The carousel is more efficient for high-turnover SKUs with sequential access. For pharma, spare parts and e-commerce with many different SKUs, the VLM is generally the best option.

faq.q: How much floor space do I save with a VLM in my warehouse?
faq.a: Typically between 70% and 90% compared to conventional shelving. A 10-meter VLM occupies 5-8 m² of floor area and stores the equivalent of 30-50 m² of conventional shelving. In buildings with free height, storage density per floor m² multiplies between 4 and 8 times.

faq.q: How does the VLM integrate with my existing ERP or WMS?
faq.a: Via standard RESTful APIs or DELIE's WMS interface. If you already have a third-party WMS (SAP EWM, Manhattan, etc.), the VLM can operate as a slave subsystem that receives extraction orders. If you don't have a WMS, DELIE's module manages warehouse inventory autonomously with ERP integration.

faq.q: Can the VLM or vertical carousel operate in temperature-controlled environments?
faq.a: The vertical carousel operates between -5°C and +40°C, compatible with positive cold rooms. The VLM has versions for environments between 2°C and 25°C, ideal for pharma and refrigerated products. For negative cold (frozen storage), DELIE's specialized AS/RS stacker cranes are recommended.

faq.q: What type of conveyor do I need for 3,000 kg pallets in my warehouse?
faq.a: For 3,000 kg pallet loads, DELIE's chain or roller conveyor is the right choice. Both are sized for that load with a frequency inverter for smooth start. The choice between chain and rollers depends on pallet fragility and whether you need zone accumulation in the warehouse.

faq.q: Can I integrate existing conveyors in my warehouse with the DELIE system?
faq.a: In many cases, yes. DELIE's WCS can control conveyors from other brands with standard control interfaces (PLC with Profibus, Profinet or similar). STOKA performs a compatibility audit during the technical diagnosis to determine which existing conveyors can be reused to reduce total investment.

faq.q: What maintenance do DELIE conveyors require?
faq.a: DELIE conveyors have quarterly preventive maintenance: chain or roller lubrication, tensioner verification and sensor check. Wear components are standardized and available in Argentina. The WCS monitors each warehouse section and issues alerts before a failure occurs.

faq.q: Do DELIE conveyors work at negative temperatures in cold rooms?
faq.a: Yes. Cold chain conveyors operate down to -25°C with special lubricants, anti-freeze materials and hermetic design. They integrate with DELIE's cold line stacker cranes and shuttle robots to create a fully automated cold storage system.

faq.q: How long does it take to implement the WMS in my warehouse?
faq.a: Base WMS implementation takes between 4 and 12 weeks depending on ERP integration complexity. In projects with DELIE hardware, the WMS is configured simultaneously with mechanical installation, not adding time to the overall schedule. STOKA manages software implementation as part of the turnkey project.

faq.q: Can the WMS manage a manual warehouse (without robots)?
faq.a: Yes. The WMS can be deployed in a manual warehouse as a first digitization step, before automating. It manages real-time inventory, list-based or pick-to-light picking, FIFO/FEFO and shift reports. This approach lets the team learn the system before adding automated hardware.

faq.q: How does the technical WMS integration with SAP or ERP work?
faq.a: DELIE has native connectors for SAP (BAPI/RFC and SAP EWM), Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics and RESTful APIs for any other system. Integration includes item and location master synchronization, inbound/outbound orders and real-time confirmations. STOKA delivers complete technical documentation.

faq.q: What happens to warehouse data if the WMS server goes down?

faq.q: Is DELIE an alternative to AutoStore in Argentina?
faq.a: Yes. DELIE manufactures ASRS cube storage, shuttle and stacker crane systems that compete directly with AutoStore. The main difference is price: DELIE systems are 30-50% more affordable than AutoStore for equivalent projects, with local technical support in Argentina through STOKA.

faq.q: What is the technical difference between AutoStore and DELIE systems?
faq.a: AutoStore uses grid robots operating on a modular grid. DELIE offers shuttle and stacker crane systems that in many configurations achieve comparable or superior densities, especially in warehouses with more than 8 meters of available height. DELIE also handles larger and heavier SKUs.

faq.q: How much does an AutoStore alternative system cost in Argentina?
faq.a: A DELIE system equivalent to a medium AutoStore installation (1,000–3,000 positions) in Argentina can cost between USD 600,000 and USD 1,500,000 complete (equipment + racking + WCS + integration). AutoStore typically costs 30-50% more for equivalent configurations, plus the cost of imported technical support.

faq.q: Does STOKA have experience installing systems in operations similar to AutoStore?
faq.a: Yes. STOKA has installed DELIE systems in e-commerce, 3PL logistics, pharma and manufacturing operations in Argentina with throughput profiles similar to those typically automated with AutoStore. We can show sector references at the first technical meeting.

beneficiosFiscales.rimiIntro: The Manufacturing and Industry Incentive Regime (RIMI), created by Ley 27.802 Title XXIII and regulated by Decreto 242/2026 and Joint General Resolution ARCA 5849/2026, allows Argentine SMEs to depreciate capital goods in 2 years (instead of their accounting useful life) and recover paid VAT in advance. It applies to companies holding a valid MiPyME certificate.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiCalificaP: Yes. ASRS systems —stacker cranes, shuttles, miniloads, carousels and palletizing robots— qualify as Capital Goods (BK) or Technological Infrastructure Goods (BIT) under the RIMI classification. Warehouse management software (WMS) and the warehouse control system (WCS) qualify as BIT when acquired as an integral part of the project.

rimiCalificaItems.label: WMS / WCS integrated into the project

beneficiosFiscales.rimiB1H3: Benefit 1 — Accelerated depreciation in Income Tax
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB1P: Instead of depreciating the asset over its accounting useful life (typically 5 to 10 years), RIMI allows deducting 100% of the acquisition value in 2 equal annual installments of Income Tax. The actual saving depends on the rate applicable to the company.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB1Ej2: USD 1,000,000 is deducted in the first fiscal year (35% rate → ~USD 350,000 saving in Income Tax).
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB1Ej3: deduction of USD 200,000/year → ~USD 70,000 saving in Income Tax over the same period.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB2P: The VAT paid on the import or local purchase of capital goods can be recovered within 9 months: ARCA/AFIP has 3 months to process the request and 6 additional months to make the refund. The credit can be applied against other national taxes, collected in cash or transferred to third parties.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB2Ej1: Import of an ASRS system for USD 2,000,000.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB2Ej2: VAT at 21% on CIF value: ≈ USD 420,000 recoverable in ≤9 months.
beneficiosFiscales.rimiB2Ej3: Without RIMI, that tax credit is gradually absorbed against future debits, immobilizing working capital for years.

rimiLimits.title: Budget quota
rimiLimits.desc: RIMI operates with an annual quota assigned by ARCA. 50% of the quota is reserved for projects approved in previous rounds; the remaining 50% is assigned by order of submission. We recommend starting the process in the first quarter of the fiscal year.

rimiLimits.title: SGI (Incentive Management System)
rimiLimits.desc: The filing platform may have closing or maintenance periods. Check the current opening window on the ARCA website before starting the documentation.

rimiLimits.title: Equity seniority
rimiLimits.desc: The company must prove at least 2 years of activity with positive net worth at the time of submission. Recently incorporated companies may not qualify.

beneficiosFiscales.rimiLegal: Regulations: Ley 27.802 Title XXIII · Decreto 242/2026 · Joint GR ARCA 5849/2026. Applicability depends on the tax profile and project structure. Consult your accountant.
beneficiosFiscales.rigiH2: RIGI — If your project exceeds USD 200 million
beneficiosFiscales.rigiIntro: The Large Investment Incentive Regime (RIGI), created by Ley 27.742 and regulated by Decreto 749/2024, is designed for strategic projects of USD 200M or more in mining, oil and gas, energy, technology and infrastructure. A typical ASRS system (USD 500K–5M) does not reach that threshold on its own — but if your company already has a project enrolled in RIGI, the warehouse automation component can be included as part of the approved promoted investment.
beneficiosFiscales.rigiIntro2: To date, 27 projects worth over USD 25,000M have been submitted. The regime was extended until July 2027. Enrolled provinces include: Mendoza, San Juan, Neuquén, Salta, Río Negro, Chubut, Santa Cruz, CABA, Córdoba and Buenos Aires, among others.
beneficiosFiscales.rigiKeyP: If your company has a project enrolled in RIGI, your ASRS system is part of the promoted investment package. We help you structure the technical documentation of the automation component so it is correctly included in the approved file.
beneficiosFiscales.rigiLegal: Regulations: Ley 27.742 · Decreto 749/2024. Enrollment in RIGI is voluntary and entails minimum investment commitments. Verify eligibility with a specialized legal advisor.
beneficiosFiscales.arancelH2: Reduced import tariffs for capital goods
beneficiosFiscales.arancelIntro: Decreto 513/2025 established the tariff reduction for 27 categories of capital goods, including logistics and industrial automation systems. The general tariff for new imported machinery ranges between 0% and 14%, depending on the NCM classification of the component.
beneficiosFiscales.arancelKeyP: There are no Argentine manufacturers of stacker cranes or ASRS systems. This enables the "capital good not produced locally" argument to classify the equipment under the NCM position with a 0% tariff, provided the classification is validated by the customs broker before importation.
beneficiosFiscales.arancelWarn: The exact tariff depends on the NCM classification of each component. We help you verify it with your customs broker before importation to maximize the benefit.
beneficiosFiscales.arancelLegal: Regulations: Decreto 513/2025 (National Executive Branch). Validity subject to annual review. Verify NCM classification with an authorized broker.
beneficiosFiscales.ivaH2: VAT reduced to 10.5% for capital goods
beneficiosFiscales.ivaIntro: The VAT Law (art. 28, Ley 23.966) sets a differential rate of 10.5% —instead of the general 21%— for capital goods included in the specific Executive Branch list. Applicability depends on the NCM tariff classification of the equipment imported or acquired locally.
beneficiosFiscales.ivaWarn: Does not apply if the operation is structured via leasing. When the acquisition is channeled through a leasing contract, the VAT on installments is settled at the general 21% rate.
beneficiosFiscales.ivaLegal: Regulations: Ley 23.966 art. 28 · Annex of capital goods with reduced rate. Applicability depends on the NCM classification of the equipment.

beneficiosFiscales.ctx2026H2: Why 2026 is a window of opportunity to automate
ctx2026P: The convergence of three factors makes 2025–2026 a unique window to invest in warehouse automation in Argentina. For the first time in decades, long-term regulatory stability, reduction of import tariffs and access to preferential financing combine simultaneously.

RIGI offers 30 years of tax stability: Income Tax at 25%, fixed tariffs and free availability of foreign currency from the third year. For companies automating today, this horizon of certainty makes it easier to calculate the 10-year TCO and present the project to the board or financiers.

Decreto 513/2025 significantly reduces the tariff based on the NCM classification: on a USD 800,000 project, it can represent up to USD 120,000 less in initial outlay. With no additional management by the company: it applies automatically when importing DELIE equipment.

The combination of BICE + RIMI + 0% tariff transforms the investment equation: the monthly financing installment falls below the monthly labor cost being replaced, making the system pay for itself from the first year of operation.

faqItems.q: Does an imported ASRS system qualify as a capital good for RIMI?
faqItems.a: Yes. Decreto 242/2026 includes new imported movable goods classified as Capital Goods (BK). An ASRS system (stacker cranes, robots, conveyors) fits this definition. WMS/WCS software could be classified as an Informatics and Telecommunications Good (BIT).

faqItems.q: How much can I really save?
faqItems.a: It depends on the amount, your company's tax structure and the applicable regimes. As a reference: on a USD 2M project, RIMI's accelerated depreciation can represent an estimated financial saving of USD 280,000 in the first 2 years, and the reduced VAT rate a direct saving of USD 210,000 if the 10.5% rate applies.

faqItems.q: Does STOKA handle the tax procedure?
faqItems.a: No. STOKA supports with the equipment's technical documentation (datasheets, NCM classifications, capital good classification). Enrollment in RIMI/RIGI and the tax procedures are handled by your accountant or tax advisor.

It is a legal gray area. RIMI requires the asset to be in the beneficiary's equity for 2 years; in leasing, ownership belongs to the bank. We recommend consulting your tax advisor to define the optimal structure.

faqItems.q: What if my company is not an SME?
faqItems.a: If you are not an SME and your project does not reach USD 200M (RIGI), you can still take advantage of: reduced tariffs, VAT 10.5% (if the NCM qualifies), leasing and provincial benefits depending on your location.

faqItems.q: Do RIGI benefits apply to the ASRS within a mining or oil project?
faqItems.a: Yes. If your company has a project enrolled in RIGI, the project's capital goods (including an ASRS) enjoy full exemption from import duties, VAT with tax credit certificates and accelerated depreciation.

faqItems.q: How much does it cost to automate a warehouse?
faqItems.a: It depends on the technology, the required throughput and the warehouse size. As a reference: a VLM module or a picking system can start from USD 150,000; a compact shuttle system is around USD 600,000; and a complete unit-load ASRS with software can exceed USD 3,000,000. In Argentina, tax benefits (RIMI, reduced tariff by NCM, 10.5% VAT and BICE financing) can reduce the real cost by 15% to 40%.

faqItems.q: Should I automate my warehouse now or wait?
faqItems.a: The combination of benefits in force in 2025–2026 makes this a favorable window: RIGI offers 30 years of tax stability, Decreto 513/2025 reduces import tariffs based on NCM classification, and the BICE line finances up to 80% of the project over 10 years. This convergence of stability, tariff reduction and preferential financing is not always available simultaneously.

beneficiosFiscales.ctaP: Every company has a different tax structure. Our team supports you with the technical documentation of the ASRS system and connects you with the benefits that apply to your case. The final tax advice is defined by your accountant — we provide the engineering.

Tax benefits are subject to current regulations, budget quotas and eligibility requirements that may vary. Consult your accountant or tax advisor on the applicability to your particular case. Last updated: June 2026. STOKA is not responsible for decisions made based on this information without independent professional advice.

buenos-aires.introP1: The Buenos Aires Metropolitan Area (AMBA) is Argentina's main node of consumption, distribution and foreign trade. Large logistics parks —in areas such as Tigre, Garín, Pilar, Tortuguitas, Esteban Echeverría and the Panamericana / Buenos Aires–La Plata corridor— host distribution centers for retail, e-commerce, food, pharma and auto parts. Pressure on productivity per square meter is maximal: industrial land near the capital is expensive and scarce, making dense automation (AS/RS, shuttle, VLM) the natural answer to grow without relocating or expanding the footprint.
buenos-aires.introP2: In this context, logistics operators and retail chains in Greater Buenos Aires face very sharp demand peaks (hot sales, commercial dates, cyber events) that require high throughput and full traceability. STOKA supports automation projects in Buenos Aires end to end: diagnosis with flow simulation, system sizing, integration with the existing ERP/WMS, installation and after-sales support with parts available locally.

buenos-aires.faqH2: Questions about automation in Buenos Aires

faq.q: Does STOKA install automation systems in Greater Buenos Aires?
faq.a: Yes. STOKA implements ASRS systems, robots and management software in logistics parks and distribution centers across AMBA: Tigre, Garín, Pilar, Tortuguitas, Esteban Echeverría and the Panamericana corridor, among others. Technical support and parts are available locally in Argentina.

faq.q: Is it worth automating a depot in Buenos Aires given land costs?
faq.a: That is exactly where it pays off most. Industrial land near the capital is expensive and scarce, so high-density systems (AS/RS, shuttle, VLM) multiply storage capacity on the same surface, avoiding costly relocations or expansions.

faq.q: Do the systems handle metropolitan e-commerce demand peaks?
faq.a: Yes. In the diagnosis phase, STOKA runs a flow simulation that sizes the system for real demand peaks (hot sales, cyber events), guaranteeing the required throughput without oversizing the investment.

mendoza.introH2: The wine and agro-industrial hub of Cuyo
mendoza.introP1: Mendoza is Argentina's main wine center and one of the country's most relevant agro-industrial hubs. Maipú, Luján de Cuyo, San Martín, Rivadavia and the Uco Valley concentrate hundreds of wineries, bottling plants, oenological-input depots and wine export logistics. Add to this canning, dried fruit, olive oil and regional products. Seasonality is strong: during the harvest, goods flow multiplies and depots must absorb peaks without losing traceability or inventory control.
mendoza.introP2: STOKA has its operating base in Maipú, Mendoza, which translates into a concrete advantage for local projects: proximity for diagnosis, installation and after-sales support, and direct knowledge of the territory and the needs of the Cuyo wine and agro-industry. We implement AS/RS systems, shuttle robots, vertical storage (VLM) and management software adapted to seasonal operation and the region's preservation requirements.

cordoba.metaDesc: ASRS, stacker cranes and robots for warehouses in Córdoba. Automotive, metalworking and agro-industry. WMS/WCS integration with local support.
cordoba.heroSub: Argentina's second industrial hub, with a strong automotive, metalworking and agro-industrial presence. STOKA implements ASRS systems, robots and management software for Córdoba's industry and logistics.
cordoba.introP1: Córdoba is Argentina's second industrial center and a key node of the country's productive heartland. Its profile combines a strong automotive and auto-parts industry —with terminals and suppliers located in the province—, metalworking, agricultural machinery and a powerful agro-industry supported by the grain production of central Argentina. The industrial parks of the city of Córdoba, Villa María, Río Cuarto and San Francisco concentrate plants that need to manage inventories of components, raw materials and finished products with high demands for traceability and just-in-time.
cordoba.introP2: Córdoba's automotive and metalworking industry operates with complex supply chains, thousands of component SKUs and sequenced delivery requirements to the production line. STOKA implements automation systems in Córdoba designed for that environment: AS/RS for finished products and raw materials, vertical storage (VLM) for the huge variety of components and parts, and WMS/WCS software that integrates with plant planning systems. All with local technical support and parts available in Argentina.

rosario.heroSub: Argentina's main agro-export hub on the Paraná River. STOKA implements ASRS systems, robots and management software for port logistics, agro-industry and distribution in Greater Rosario, Santa Fe.
rosario.breadcrumbLabel: Warehouse automation in Rosario, Santa Fe

stats.l: industrial and port belt on the river

rosario.introP1: Rosario and the industrial belt around it —from San Lorenzo and Puerto General San Martín to Villa Gobernador Gálvez— form Argentina's main agro-export complex. On the Paraná River, port terminals, oilseed crushing plants, grain storage and dense agro-industry and logistics activity are concentrated. It is the exit point for much of the country's grain, flour and oil exports, generating enormous and highly seasonal logistics flows tied to the agricultural campaign.
rosario.introP2: In addition to agro-export activity, there is metalworking, food industry and a growing distribution sector supplying the center of the country. In this environment, the depots and logistics centers of Greater Rosario need to absorb campaign peaks, manage inputs and finished products with traceability, and operate under demanding conditions. STOKA implements high-density AS/RS systems, shuttle robots, vertical storage and WMS/WCS software in Rosario, with local technical support and parts available in Argentina.
